axiom LearnedBotany : obj → Prop
axiom UnderstoodNeeds : obj → Prop
axiom Thrive : obj → Prop
axiom SoughtAdvice : obj → Prop

axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, LearnedBotany x → UnderstoodNeeds x
-- If someone understands plant needs, their plants thrive
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, UnderstoodNeeds x → Thrive x
-- Either they learned about botany or they sought advice (or both)
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, LearnedBotany x ∨ SoughtAdvice x

-- Mary's plants did not thrive
axiom NotThriveMary : ¬ Thrive Mary

  have h_notU : ¬ UnderstoodNeeds Mary := by
    intro hU
    have hT : Thrive Mary := R2 Mary hU
    exact NotThriveMary hT
  have h_notL : ¬ LearnedBotany Mary := by
    intro hL
    have hU : UnderstoodNeeds Mary := R1 Mary hL
    exact h_notU hU
  have h_or : LearnedBotany Mary ∨ SoughtAdvice Mary := R3 Mary
  cases h_or with
  | inl hL => exact False.elim (h_notL hL)
  | inr hS => exact hS
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom RevenueImproves : obj → Prop

      exact hE
  have hMore : MoreCustomers Store := A2 Store hEvents
  exact A3 Store hMore

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Jake : obj

axiom WeatherNice : Prop
axiom RelaxOutside : obj → Prop
axiom ReadBooks : obj → Prop
axiom PackedEnough : obj → Prop
axiom Prepared : obj → Prop

-- If the weather is nice, then one can relax outside
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, WeatherNice → RelaxOutside x
-- If one reads books, then they packed enough
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ReadBooks x → PackedEnough x
-- Either the weather is nice, or they did not pack enough, or both
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, WeatherNice ∨ ¬PackedEnough x ∨ (WeatherNice ∧ ¬PackedEnough x)
-- If one is prepared, then they read books
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, Prepared x → ReadBooks x

-- From not relaxing, infer not nice weather (modus tollens on R1)
lemma no_weather_of_no_relax (x : obj) : ¬RelaxOutside x → ¬WeatherNice := by
  intro hnr
  have hw2r : WeatherNice → RelaxOutside x := R1 x
  exact mt hw2r hnr

-- From not nice weather and the disjunction R3, infer not packed enough
lemma not_enough_of_not_weather (x : obj) : ¬WeatherNice → ¬PackedEnough x := by
  intro hNotW
  have disj := R3 x
  cases disj with
  | inl hW =>
      intro _hE
      exact False.elim (hNotW hW)
  | inr h2 =>
      cases h2 with
      | inl hNotE => exact hNotE
      | inr hWandNotE =>
          exact hWandNotE.right

-- From "reads -> enough", infer "not enough -> not reads"
lemma not_read_of_not_enough (x : obj) : ¬PackedEnough x → ¬ReadBooks x := by
  intro hNotE
  exact mt (R2 x) hNotE

-- From "prepared -> reads", infer "not reads -> not prepared"
lemma not_prepared_of_not_read (x : obj) : ¬ReadBooks x → ¬Prepared x := by
  intro hNotB
  exact mt (R4 x) hNotB

-- We can show: if Jake cannot relax outside, then he is NOT prepared.
theorem not_prepared_if_cannot_relax : (¬RelaxOutside Jake) → ¬Prepared Jake := by
  intro hnr
  have hnw : ¬WeatherNice := (no_weather_of_no_relax Jake) hnr
  have hne : ¬PackedEnough Jake := (not_enough_of_not_weather Jake) hnw
  have hnb : ¬ReadBooks Jake := (not_read_of_not_enough Jake) hne
  exact (not_prepared_of_not_read Jake) hnb

-- Therefore, we cannot prove: (¬RelaxOutside Jake) → Prepared Jake
-- In fact, the opposite implication holds as shown above.
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

axiom Jake : obj

axiom WeatherNice : Prop
axiom RelaxOutside : obj → Prop
axiom ReadBooks : obj → Prop
axiom PackedEnough : obj → Prop
axiom Prepared : obj → Prop

-- If the weather is nice, then one can relax outside
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, WeatherNice → RelaxOutside x
-- If one reads books, then they packed enough
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ReadBooks x → PackedEnough x
-- Either the weather is nice, or they did not pack enough, or both
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, WeatherNice ∨ ¬PackedEnough x ∨ (WeatherNice ∧ ¬PackedEnough x)
-- If one is prepared, then they read books
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, Prepared x → ReadBooks x

-- From not relaxing, infer not nice weather (modus tollens on R1)
lemma no_weather_of_no_relax (x : obj) : ¬RelaxOutside x → ¬WeatherNice := by
  intro hnr
  exact mt (R1 x) hnr

-- From not nice weather and the disjunction R3, infer not packed enough
lemma not_enough_of_not_weather (x : obj) : ¬WeatherNice → ¬PackedEnough x := by
  intro hNotW
  intro hPE
  have h := R3 x
  cases h
  case inl hW =>
    exact hNotW hW
  case inr h2 =>
    cases h2
    case inl hNotE =>
      exact hNotE hPE
    case inr hBoth =>
      exact hBoth.right hPE

-- From "reads -> enough", infer "not enough -> not reads"
lemma not_read_of_not_enough (x : obj) : ¬PackedEnough x → ¬ReadBooks x := by
  intro hNotE
  exact mt (R2 x) hNotE

-- From "prepared -> reads", infer "not reads -> not prepared"
lemma not_prepared_of_not_read (x : obj) : ¬ReadBooks x → ¬Prepared x := by
  intro hNotB
  exact mt (R4 x) hNotB

-- If Jake cannot relax outside, then he is NOT prepared.
theorem not_prepared_if_cannot_relax : (¬RelaxOutside Jake) → ¬Prepared Jake := by
  intro hnr
  have hnw : ¬WeatherNice := no_weather_of_no_relax Jake hnr
  have hne : ¬PackedEnough Jake := not_enough_of_not_weather Jake hnw
  have hnb : ¬ReadBooks Jake := not_read_of_not_enough Jake hne
  exact not_prepared_of_not_read Jake hnb

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Alex : obj

axiom Monday : Prop
axiom Quiz : Prop

axiom Studied : obj → Prop
axiom DoWell : obj → Prop
axiom Good : obj → Prop

-- If it is Monday, then there is a quiz
axiom A1 : Monday → Quiz
-- If someone does well on the quiz, then they studied hard
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, DoWell x → Studied x
-- Either it is Monday, or they did not study hard, or both Monday and did not study hard
axiom A3 : Monday ∨ ¬Studied Alex ∨ (Monday ∧ ¬Studied Alex)
-- Someone who is good at math does well on quizzes
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Good x → DoWell x

lemma not_monday_of_no_quiz : ¬Quiz → ¬Monday :=
  fun hNoQuiz hMon => hNoQuiz (A1 hMon)

lemma not_studied_of_no_quiz : ¬Quiz → ¬Studied Alex :=
  fun hNoQuiz =>
    let hNotMon : ¬Monday := not_monday_of_no_quiz hNoQuiz
    Or.elim A3
      (fun hMon => False.elim (hNotMon hMon))
      (fun h => Or.elim h
        (fun hNS => hNS)
        (fun hAnd => And.right hAnd))

lemma not_dowell_of_not_studied : ¬Studied Alex → ¬DoWell Alex :=
  fun hNS hDW => hNS (A2 Alex hDW)

-- From no quiz, we can derive that Alex is not good at math
lemma not_good_of_no_quiz : ¬Quiz → ¬Good Alex :=
  fun hNoQuiz hGood =>
    let hNS : ¬Studied Alex := not_studied_of_no_quiz hNoQuiz
    let hNDW : ¬DoWell Alex := not_dowell_of_not_studied hNS
    hNDW (A4 Alex hGood)

-- Therefore, we cannot prove (¬Quiz → Good Alex); in fact, its negation holds.
-- theorem target : ¬Quiz → Good Alex := by
--   -- Cannot be proved from the given axioms; moreover, ¬Quiz → ¬Good Alex is derivable.
--   admit
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

iterations.llm_response:
<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom James : Person
axiom StartNew : Person → Prop
axiom Motivated : Person → Prop
axiom GymClosed : Prop
axiom CantFollow : Person → Prop
axiom Frustrated : Person → Prop
axiom Indulged : Person → Prop
axiom MightIndulge : Person → Prop

-- If someone starts a new workout routine, they will be motivated to keep it up.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : Person, StartNew x → Motivated x
-- If the gym closes for renovations, then they can't follow their routine.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : Person, GymClosed → CantFollow x
-- Either they (James) started a new workout, or the gym was closed (or both).
axiom A3 : StartNew James ∨ GymClosed
-- If they couldn't follow their routine, they would feel frustrated.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : Person, CantFollow x → Frustrated x
-- If they felt frustrated, they might indulge in some unhealthy snacks.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : Person, Frustrated x → MightIndulge x

-- Given: James was not motivated to workout.
axiom A6 : ¬ Motivated James

-- We can derive that James might indulge (but not that he did).
theorem james_might_indulge : MightIndulge James := by
  exact Or.elim A3
    (fun hstart => False.elim (A6 (A1 James hstart)))
    (fun hclosed =>
      let hcant : CantFollow James := (A2 James) hclosed
      let hfr : Frustrated James := A4 James hcant
      A5 James hfr)

-- Cannot prove Indulged James from the given axioms (only "might indulge" is given).
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

axiom MightIndulge : Person → Prop

  exact Or.elim A3

    (fun hclosed =>

      let hfr : Frustrated James := A4 James hcant
      A5 James hfr)

-- Cannot prove Indulged James from the given axioms (only "might indulge" is given).

axiom obj : Type
axiom Mary : obj

axiom Sale : Prop
axiom Payday : Prop
axiom PricesUp : Prop

axiom StockUp : obj → Prop
axiom MakeList : obj → Prop
axiom Plan : obj → Prop

-- If the supermarket has a sale, then people will stock up on groceries.
axiom A1 : Sale → ∀ x : obj, StockUp x

-- If someone makes a shopping list, then they plan what to buy.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, MakeList x → Plan x

-- Either the store has a sale, or people don't plan what to buy, or there's a sale but they don't plan ahead.
axiom A3 : Sale ∨ (∀ x : obj, ¬ Plan x) ∨ (Sale ∧ (∀ x : obj, ¬ Plan x))

-- If payday was this week, then people make a shopping list.
axiom A4 : Payday → ∀ x : obj, MakeList x

-- Either it was payday this week, or prices went up recently, or both.
axiom A5 : Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp)

theorem mary_not_stockup_implies_pricesUp : (¬ StockUp Mary) → PricesUp := by
  intro hNS
  -- From Mary's not stocking up, we get not Sale.
  have hNotSale : ¬ Sale := fun s => hNS ((A1 s) Mary)
  -- From the trichotomy, deduce that nobody plans what to buy.
  have allNotPlan : ∀ x : obj, ¬ Plan x := by
    cases A3 with
    | inl hSale =>
      exact False.elim (hNotSale hSale)
    | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hAllNotPlan => exact hAllNotPlan
      | inr hSaleAndAll => exact hSaleAndAll.right
  have notPlanMary : ¬ Plan Mary := allNotPlan Mary
  -- Payday would imply Mary plans; hence not Payday.
  have payday_implies_planMary : Payday → Plan Mary := fun p =>
    let allML := A4 p
    have mlMary : MakeList Mary := allML Mary
    exact A2 Mary mlMary
  have notPayday : ¬ Payday := fun p => notPlanMary (payday_implies_planMary p)
  -- From Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp) and not Payday, conclude PricesUp.
  have hPricesUp : PricesUp := by
    cases A5 with
    | inl hPayday => exact False.elim (notPayday hPayday)
    | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hUp => exact hUp
      | inr hBoth => exact hBoth.right
  exact hPricesUp
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom AllList : obj → Prop

  -- From H1 and A1 specialized to John, we get ¬Sale

  have nAll : ¬ AllList John := by
    cases A3 with

  -- From A5, deduce SoldOut John (using contradiction if we get BeginMonth)

        exact A2 John hGJ
      have : False := nAll hAll
      exact False.elim this

      | inr hBoth => exact hBoth.right

axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Stirs x → Thick
-- Either someone adds salt to the stew or the stew does not thicken perfectly (or both)

  -- No one added salt (since that would make the stew savory)
  have hno_salt : ¬ (∃ x : obj, AddsSalt x) := by
    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro w hw =>
      exact hns (R1 w hw)
  -- Therefore, by the law, the stew does not thicken
  have hnot_thick : ¬ Thick := by
    cases Law1 with
    | inl hexsalt =>
      have fals : False := hno_salt hexsalt
      exact (fun _ => False.elim fals)
    | inr hnt => exact hnt
  -- Hence, no one stirs (since stirring implies thickening)
  have hno_stirs : ¬ (∃ x : obj, Stirs x) := by

    cases hex with

  have hno_tastes : ¬ (∃ x : obj, Tastes x) := by
    intro hex
    cases hex with
    | intro w hw =>
      exact hno_stirs ⟨w, R3 w hw⟩

  cases Secret with

axiom BedLate : Person → Prop

axiom R_miss_late : ∀ x : Person, MissBus x → Late x

axiom R_bed_up : ∀ x : Person, BedLate x → UpLate x

  -- From knowledge factivity, get ¬TimeReady

  -- From Alarm → TimeReady and ¬TimeReady, derive ¬Alarm (modus tollens)
  have h_not_alarm : ¬ Alarm :=

  -- Conclude ¬BedLate John by contradiction with ¬Late John
  intro hbl

  exact h_not_late hl

            exact False.elim (notW w)

axiom WeekendComing : Prop

axiom FinishTasks : obj → Prop
axiom ClearMind : obj → Prop
axiom Organized : obj → Prop
axiom ToDoList : obj → Prop

-- If it is the weekend coming up, then they (anyone) relax.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, WeekendComing → Relax x
-- If someone finishes their tasks, then they have a clear mind.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, FinishTasks x → ClearMind x
-- Either the weekend is coming, or they (anyone) do not have a clear mind.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, WeekendComing ∨ ¬ ClearMind x
-- If someone is organized, then they finish their tasks.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, Organized x → FinishTasks x
-- If they made a to-do list, then they are organized.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, ToDoList x → Organized x

theorem todo_implies_clearmind : ∀ x : obj, ToDoList x → ClearMind x :=
  fun x => fun tl => R2 x (R4 x (R5 x tl))

-- Given that Alex does not relax this weekend, we can show Alex did not make a to-do list.
theorem no_todo_from_not_relax : (¬ Relax Alex) → ¬ ToDoList Alex :=
  fun HnotRelax =>
    -- From HnotRelax and R1, infer that the weekend is not coming.
    let notW : ¬ WeekendComing :=
      fun w =>
        let relaxA : Relax Alex := R1 Alex w
        HnotRelax relaxA
    -- From R3 and notW, infer Alex does not have a clear mind.
    let notCM : ¬ ClearMind Alex :=
      match R3 Alex with
      | Or.inl w =>
          by
            intro cm
            exact False.elim (notW w)
      | Or.inr ncm => ncm
    -- Conclude Alex did not make a to-do list.
    by
      intro tl
      exact notCM (todo_implies_clearmind Alex tl)

axiom Hyper : Prop

-- If the cafeteria serves pizza for lunch, the kids will be hyper all afternoon.

axiom T1 : Sub ∨ Pizza
-- If the students goof off in class, they won't learn much that day.

-- If they don't learn much, they'll have lots of homework.

  intro nh

  have np : ¬Pizza := by
    intro p
    have h : Hyper := R2 p
    exact nh h
  -- From Sub ∨ Pizza and ¬Pizza, deduce Sub
  have hs : Sub := by
    cases T1 with
    | inl hs => exact hs
    | inr hp => exact False.elim (np hp)
  -- Chain the implications to get Homework
  have g : Goof := R1 hs
  have nl : ¬Learn := R3 g
  exact R4 nl
</lean>

<lean>
axiom Watered : Prop
axiom Saturated : Prop
axiom Rain : Prop
axiom Bloom : Prop
axiom Fragrance : Prop
axiom Attracts : Prop

-- If the garden is watered regularly, the flowers will bloom.
axiom A1 : Watered → Bloom
-- If it rains a lot, the soil becomes saturated.
axiom A2 : Rain → Saturated
-- Either the garden is watered routinely, or the soil did not saturate.
axiom A3 : Watered ∨ ¬ Saturated
-- If the flowers bloom, they will release fragrance.
axiom A4 : Bloom → Fragrance
-- If there is fragrance, it will attract butterflies.
axiom A5 : Fragrance → Attracts

theorem heavy_rain_attracts : Rain → Attracts := by
  intro hr
  have hs : Saturated := A2 hr
  have hw : Watered := Or.elim A3 (fun hw => hw) (fun hns => False.elim (hns hs))
  have hb : Bloom := A1 hw
  have hf : Fragrance := A4 hb
  exact A5 hf
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes
